verifyCommand: uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .
acceptanceCriteria: Verification passes (uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .)
All non-skipped features completed (passes: true)
All pre-existing tests still pass unchanged
notes: Loop 1 of the research-driven detector batch (see deep-research findings in this session). Build TWO new public-data detectors that target fraud patterns DIFFERENT from the dollars-per-job ratio already covered: (A) amount_anomaly (round-number + payroll-cap-maximization 'bunching') and (B) multiple_funded_loans (entity resolution → borrowers with more funded loans than the program's per-borrower draw limits allow). CRITICAL METHODOLOGY (the H6 lesson): BOTH detectors register in registry.exploratory_detectors(), NOT all_detectors() — they are NOT in the default composite. Promotion into the composite is a MANUAL human decision after validating real-data lift, which happens AFTER this loop. CRITICAL GUARDRAILS: (1) NEVER run the relief-probe CLI or open data/relief_probe.duckdb (the real 11.3M-loan warehouse); all tests build their own warehouse via warehouse.connect(tmp_path/...). (2) Do NOT hand-write or invent benchmark/lift NUMBERS in README/docs — qualitative only; real numbers are generated by a human post-loop. (3) Entity key is loan_number (str). (4) REUSE existing modules: detectors/base.py (Detector/Signal), detectors/_address.py (normalize_address), labels/resolve.py (normalize_name), payroll_cap.py (per-employee cap constants $20,833 / $29,167 for NAICS 72). Detectors are READ-ONLY. (5) Match codebase style: from __future__ import annotations, typed, module docstrings, ruff line-length 90. Commit ONE feature per iteration; set passes=true; update plans/progress.md; re-read plans/guardrails.md every iteration. Data facts: processing_method is 'PPP' (first draw) or 'PPS' (second draw); a borrower may legitimately hold at most one of each.

features.title: Entity-resolution key helper (normalized name + building address)
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/_entity.py with entity_key(borrower_name, address, city, state, zip_code) -> str | None that combines labels.resolve.normalize_name(name) with detectors._address.normalize_address(...) into one canonical key for 'the same borrower at the same building'
Returns None when either the normalized name or the normalized address is missing/blank (so unkeyable loans are excluded from grouping rather than mis-merged)
Reuses the existing normalize_name and normalize_address — does NOT reimplement suffix/address normalization

tests/test_entity.py: same borrower at variously-formatted spellings of one address map to the SAME key ('Acme LLC' @ '1 Main St' == 'ACME' @ '1 MAIN STREET'); different borrowers or different buildings map to DIFFERENT keys; blank name or blank address -> None; pure function (no warehouse access)
Verification passes
features.notes: Thin composition of two existing normalizers. normalize_name is in src/relief_probe/labels/resolve.py; normalize_address in src/relief_probe/detectors/_address.py.

features.title: amount_anomaly detector (round-number + payroll-cap bunching)
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/amount_anomaly.py defining AmountAnomalyDetector(Detector) with detector_id='amount_anomaly' and a one-line summary

Flags loans whose current_approval_amount looks fabricated/reverse-engineered rather than payroll-derived, via two documented sub-signals: (a) ROUND-NUMBER — amount is an exact round multiple (e.g. divisible by 1,000 / 5,000 / 10,000), graded so rounder is higher; (b) CAP-MAXIMIZATION ('bunching') — implied per-employee loan (amount / jobs_reported, jobs>=1) sits at/just-below the program per-employee ceiling ($20,833 general; $29,167 for NAICS starting '72'), i.e. the borrower claimed the maximum allowable salary for every employee

Reuses the per-employee cap constant(s) from payroll_cap.py rather than hardcoding new ones; cap-maximization is the AT/just-below-cap band, distinct from payroll_cap_exceedance which flags ABOVE the cap (so the two do not double-count the same band)
score is comparable WITHIN the detector and monotonic in how strongly the sub-signals fire; evidence dict records which sub-signals fired and the implied per-employee amount / roundness
Detector is read-only

tests/test_amount_anomaly.py on a seeded tmp_path warehouse: a perfectly round, cap-maxed loan fires; a normal payroll-derived amount (odd cents, mid-cohort per-employee) does NOT; a loan ABOVE the cap is left to payroll_cap (not the focus here); evidence reports the fired sub-signals
Verification passes
features.notes: Per-loan, no external data. Round numbers + cap-bunching are classic fabrication tells (Benford-adjacent). Keep the cap math consistent with payroll_cap.py. Document the false-positive mode (some legitimate loans are genuinely round / genuinely at the cap).

features.title: multiple_funded_loans detector (duplicate funding via entity resolution)
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/multiple_funded_loans.py defining MultipleFundedLoansDetector(Detector) with detector_id='multiple_funded_loans' and a one-line summary

Groups loans by entity_key (L1-001); a borrower may legitimately hold at most one first-draw (processing_method='PPP') and one second-draw ('PPS'), so flags entities that EXCEED that: >=2 loans of the SAME processing_method, or >2 funded loans total

Every loan belonging to a flagged entity emits a Signal scored monotonically in the excess loan count (and optionally total dollars); evidence includes entity_key, n_loans, per-draw counts, total_amount, and the loan_numbers in the group
Loans with an unkeyable entity (entity_key None) are skipped; read-only

tests/test_multiple_funded_loans.py on a seeded tmp_path warehouse: one borrower with two PPP (same-draw) loans fires; a borrower with exactly one PPP + one PPS (legit) does NOT; three+ loans for one resolved entity fires; distinct borrowers each with a single loan stay quiet; this is DISTINCT from the duplicate_address_ring detector (same RESOLVED borrower with many loans, not many distinct borrowers at one address)
Verification passes
features.notes: GAO-validated indicator (~22k recipients with multiple funded loans). The precise version of the address idea: resolve the ENTITY, count its funded loans against the 1-per-draw rule. Reuse entity_key from L1-001 and processing_method (PPP/PPS).

features.title: Register both as EXPLORATORY (not in the default composite) + integration
acceptanceCriteria: registry.exploratory_detectors() includes AmountAnomalyDetector() and MultipleFundedLoansDetector() (alongside DuplicateAddressRingDetector); all_detectors() is UNCHANGED (still only the two validated $/job detectors) — new detectors are NOT auto-promoted into the composite
get_detector(id) resolves both new detector_ids; update the registry module docstring

tests: both new ids are in exploratory_detectors() and NOT in all_detectors(); run_all(con, detectors=[*all_detectors(), *exploratory_detectors()]) on a seeded warehouse returns counts including 'amount_anomaly' and 'multiple_funded_loans'; default run_all(con) does NOT include them
Verification passes
features.notes: Mirror the H6 disposition exactly: exploratory_detectors() is the holding area until a human validates real-data lift and promotes. Do NOT touch all_detectors().

features.title: Docs: catalog the two new exploratory detectors (qualitative only)
acceptanceCriteria: README detector catalog lists amount_anomaly and multiple_funded_loans as EXPLORATORY detectors (built + tested, not yet in the default composite, pending real-data validation); cite the motivation (Griffin et al. round-number/cap-bunching; GAO multiple-funded-loans) and honest false-positive modes; NO benchmark/lift numbers

NEXT_STEPS.md: add a 'Loop 1' entry under the detector backlog noting both detectors built as exploratory, with a MANUAL post-loop step to score+benchmark on the real warehouse and promote any that show lift
No fabricated metrics anywhere; qualitative text only
Verification passes
features.notes: Docs only. Honest framing: these are candidates pending validation, exactly like the ring detector was before it was tested.
